summary: Matched a broad set of gm_17C0 All-Star/game-mode routines and improved several ground.c stage/light/fog routines. The decomp.dev report recorded +5104 matched bytes, +0.13% overall matched code, 16 new 100% matches, and 4 partial-match improvements. The key enablers were replacing raw UNK_T/M2C field access with typed UnkAllstarData layouts, adding precise helper prototypes, splitting/typing Ground_803E06C8 light data in symbols, and factoring repeated ground allocation/joint-loading patterns into small helpers.
what_changed: Implemented multiple previously stubbed gm_17C0 functions, including fn_8017C0C8, fn_8017C1A4, fn_8017C71C, fn_8017C7A0, fn_8017C7EC, gm_8017C838, gm_8017C984, fn_8017DEC8, fn_8017DF28, fn_8017DF90, gm_8017DFF4, gm_8017E068, fn_8017E0E4, and fn_8017E160.
Changed gm_17C0 All-Star data storage from raw UNK_T arrays to typed static struct UnkAllstarData instances for lbl_80472C30 and lbl_80472CB0.
Expanded struct UnkAllstarData in src/melee/gm/types.h from a 0xA0 byte pad into named byte fields x0 through xB plus nested struct UnkAllstarData_xC and trailing padding.
Updated gm_17C0.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS style declarations to concrete return and parameter types for newly matched routines, including struct UnkAllstarData* accessors.
Moved lbl_80472D28_t, lbl_80472E48_t, and static lbl_804D4190 ownership out of gm_1601.static.h into gm_17C0.c for the matching unit that uses them.
Adjusted gm_16AE.c call sites to use typed struct UnkAllstarData* access, such as temp_r30->x0 and fn_8017DEC8(1)->x8 instead of byte-pointer indexing.
Added CostumeListsForeachCharacter[FTKIND_MAX] in ft_0852.c, referencing many existing UnkCostumeStruct symbols and their costume counts.
In ground.c, added alloc_user_data_ground and get_jobj_inline helpers, switched hard-coded "ground.c" assert/report strings to __FILE__, and reused the helpers in Ground_801C14D0 and Ground_801C1A20.

Implemented or substantially improved ground light setup around Ground_801C466C, including Ground_801C466C_inline, typed Ground_803E06C8 as HSD_LightDesc*[3], and typed Ground_801C49B4 as returning HSD_LightDesc**.
Reworked Ground_801C4FAC fog distance calculations with a temporary Vec3 delta and simplified phi_f30 handling, improving the partial match.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt so Ground_803E06C8 shrank from size 0x68 to 0xC and following data was split into lbl_803E06D4, lbl_803E06F0, and lbl_803E070C.
Typed grPushOn_80219230 as int grPushOn_80219230(int) and lbBgFlash_800205F0/8002063C/80020688 as concrete void-return functions with explicit parameters.
smart_moves: Replacing raw buffers and M2C_FIELD access with a real UnkAllstarData layout let several gm_17C0 helpers collapse into simple field reads and switch-based accessors.

Factoring repeated HSD_MemAlloc/Ground userdata allocation and scaled joint-loading code into helpers reduced local stack noise and helped Ground_801C14D0 and Ground_801C1A20 reach or improve matches.
Splitting the Ground_803E06C8 data symbol in symbols.txt aligned the binary data with actual light descriptor pointer data and adjacent strings instead of treating a 0x68 byte range as one object.
Localizing static structs/statics to gm_17C0.c avoided depending on gm_1601.static.h for data that belonged to the matched translation unit.
Typing cross-module prototypes in grpushon.h and lbbgflash.h enabled gm_17C0 call sites to express simple direct calls without UNK_PARAMS casts or pointer arithmetic.
The ground light setup match used a fallback static HSD_LightDesc* array when stage-specific light data lookup returned NULL, preserving observed control flow while making types explicit.
decomp_lessons: For game-mode data blocks, even a mostly unnamed byte-field struct is better than a raw UNK_T array when multiple functions index fixed offsets; it exposes shared semantics and unlocks accessor matches.

When a data object appears too large in symbols.txt and contains strings or different object types after the true object, split it into smaller symbols before forcing C declarations to fit the old size.
Repeated compiler patterns in stage code can often be recovered by introducing tiny helpers for allocation or common HSD object construction; this can reduce stack layout mismatches.
Use __FILE__ in assert/OSReport macros when the matched code expects compiler-emitted file strings; hard-coded file string objects can disturb data layout and matching.

Concrete header prototypes are not just cleanup: they improve call-site codegen across modules, especially for routines like lbBgFlash_8002063C(int), lbBgFlash_80020688(int), and grPushOn_80219230(int).
A nested struct for a fixed-offset sub-block, as with UnkAllstarData.xC, can express repeated subfield checks such as x18/x24/x30 while retaining unknown field names until semantics are known.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-based decomp names for unknown functions and data: fn_8017C1A4, gm_8017C838, Ground_801C466C, lbl_80472C30, and Ground_803E06C8.

Used subsystem prefixes consistently: gm_ for game-mode routines, Ground_ for stage/ground routines, ft_ for fighter data, lbBgFlash_ for background flash helpers, and grPushOn_ for Push On stage helpers.
For unknown structs, retained address/role placeholders such as struct UnkAllstarData, struct UnkAllstarData_xC, struct lbl_80472D28_t, and struct lbl_804706C0_t.
For partially understood fields, used offset-like names such as x0, x1, x8, xC, x18, x24, and x30 rather than inventing semantic names without evidence.
Helper names in ground.c were descriptive but local to observed behavior: alloc_user_data_ground, get_jobj_inline, Ground_801C466C_inline, and vec_len.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted gm_8017E424 and gm_8017EB30 return types to struct UnkAllstarData* so accessors like gm_8017E430 could read lbl_80472C30.x3 directly instead of M2C_FIELD.
Used switch statements over gm_801A4310 and explicit cases 3, 4, and 5 in fn_8017DF28/fn_8017DF90 to map mode state to All-Star data sources and mode indices.
In Ground_801C14D0 and Ground_801C1A20, helper extraction removed repeated stack HSD_Joint temporaries and centralized the HSD_JObjLoadJoint null-check/report loop.
Adjusted Ground_801C4640 signature to accept an unused int parameter, matching the GX link callback signature used by GObj_SetupGXLink.
Typed Ground_801C49B4 as HSD_LightDesc** and returned Ground_803E06C8 directly rather than &Ground_803E06C8, matching the pointer-level expected by light object loading.
Reordered and grouped vector component scaling assignments in Ground_801C4FAC to better match compiler scheduling while preserving arithmetic.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported 16 new matches and 4 unmatched-function improvements for GALE01 from base 6ff888e to head 721a567.
Bot-reported new 100% matches included gm_17C0 fn_8017C1A4 (+1400 bytes), gm_8017C838 (+332), fn_8017C0C8 (+220), Ground_801C14D0 (+204), fn_8017C71C (+132), and gm_801A51A8_OnLoad (+28).
Bot-reported partial improvements included Ground_801C466C to 82.09%, Ground_801C4FAC to 87.50%, fn_8017E160 to 93.62%, and Ground_801C36F4 to 89.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or semantically type struct UnkAllstarData fields x0/x1/x2/x8/xC.
Check follow-up work on Ground_801C466C to see how the remaining 82.09% match was completed.
Search for later symbol cleanup around Ground_803E06C8, lbl_803E06D4, lbl_803E06F0, and lbl_803E070C.
Look for later decomp of gm_17C0 functions still stubbed after this PR, especially fn_8017C9A8 and neighboring All-Star helpers.
Search for later semantic naming of CostumeListsForeachCharacter and UnkCostumeStruct entries in ft_0852.c.
